description: Platform Features — Auth (Clerk), Stripe Pro subscriptions, Claude Vision photo diagnosis, Google Maps yard estimator, multi-lawn + household accounts

userStories.title: Install Clerk and configure environment variables
userStories.description: As a developer, I need Clerk installed and configured so authentication can be added to the app.
acceptanceCriteria: Install @clerk/nextjs package

Add env vars to .env.local (placeholder values): NEXT_PUBLIC_CLERK_PUBLISHABLE_KEY, CLERK_SECRET_KEY, NEXT_PUBLIC_CLERK_SIGN_IN_URL=/sign-in, NEXT_PUBLIC_CLERK_SIGN_UP_URL=/sign-up, NEXT_PUBLIC_CLERK_AFTER_SIGN_IN_URL=/dashboard, NEXT_PUBLIC_CLERK_AFTER_SIGN_UP_URL=/onboarding/1
Add <ClerkProvider> wrapping {children} in src/app/layout.tsx (import from @clerk/nextjs)
env.local.example updated with new Clerk key names (no values)
Typecheck passes
Deploy to Vercel and confirm build succeeds (Clerk keys must also be set in Vercel environment variables)

userStories.title: Replace middleware passthrough with Clerk auth middleware
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the middleware to enforce authentication on protected routes.
acceptanceCriteria: Replace src/middleware.ts content: import clerkMiddleware and createRouteMatcher from @clerk/nextjs/server
Public routes (no auth required): /, /sign-in(.*), /sign-up(.*), /api/zone(.*), /onboarding(.*)
All other routes including /dashboard, /profile, /diagnose, /estimate require auth
Unauthenticated access to /dashboard redirects to /sign-in
matcher config exported: ['/((?!_next|[^?]*\.(?:html?|css|js(?!on)|jpe?g|webp|png|gif|svg|ttf|woff2?|ico|csv|docx?|xlsx?|zip|webmanifest)).*)','/(api|trpc)(.*)']
Typecheck passes
Deploy to Vercel; verify /dashboard redirects to /sign-in when logged out

userStories.title: Add users table linked to Clerk user_id
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a users table so we can store subscription status and profile data server-side linked to real accounts.
acceptanceCriteria: Run migration: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS users (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, clerk_user_id TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL, email TEXT, subscription_status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'free', stripe_customer_id TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW())
Create src/lib/user.ts with getOrCreateUser(clerkUserId: string, email: string) server function — upserts into users table, returns user row
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create sign-in and sign-up pages with Clerk components
userStories.description: As a user, I want dedicated sign-in and sign-up pages that match the app's dark theme.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/app/sign-in/[[...sign-in]]/page.tsx — renders <SignIn /> from @clerk/nextjs centered on page with dark background
Create src/app/sign-up/[[...sign-up]]/page.tsx — renders <SignUp /> from @clerk/nextjs centered on page
Both pages use <main className='flex items-center justify-center min-h-screen'> wrapper with backgroundColor: 'var(--color-background)'
Clerk appearance prop sets dark theme: appearance={{ variables: { colorBackground: '#0d1a0d', colorInputBackground: '#1a2e1a', colorPrimary: '#4caf50', colorText: '#e8f5e9' } }}
Typecheck passes
Deploy to Vercel; visit /sign-in and confirm Clerk sign-in widget renders with dark styling
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add Sign In / Sign Up buttons to ClientNav and landing page
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want to see sign-in and sign-up options in the navigation so I can access my account.
acceptanceCriteria: In src/app/ClientNav.tsx, import SignedIn, SignedOut, UserButton from @clerk/nextjs
Desktop nav when SignedOut: show 'Sign In' link (href=/sign-in) and 'Sign Up' button (href=/sign-up, primary button styling)
Desktop nav when SignedIn: show <UserButton afterSignOutUrl='/' /> on the right side
Landing page hero section: when SignedOut, add Sign In and Sign Up links below the existing CTA button
Mobile bottom tab bar: only shown when SignedIn; SignedOut users on / see no bottom nav
Typecheck passes
Deploy and verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Update profile actions to use Clerk user_id instead of session_id
userStories.description: As a developer, I need profile save/load to use the real Clerk user ID so data persists across sessions.
acceptanceCriteria: In src/actions/profile.ts, import auth from @clerk/nextjs/server
Update getProfile: call auth() to get userId; if no userId return null; query by clerk_user_id = userId
Update saveProfile: get userId from auth(); upsert with clerk_user_id as the key
Remove session_id parameter from both functions' signatures
Update all callers in src/app/profile/page.tsx — remove getSessionId() calls, no longer pass session_id
Typecheck passes
Deploy to Vercel; sign in, save profile, sign out, sign back in — verify profile data persisted

userStories.title: Migrate onboarding final step to save profile for authenticated users
userStories.description: As an authenticated user completing onboarding, I want my profile saved to my account automatically.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/actions/onboarding.ts with saveOnboardingProfile(data: { zip_code, state, usda_zone, grass_type, square_footage, has_pets, sun_exposure }) server action — calls getOrCreateUser then upserts into user profile
In the onboarding done/confirmation step (Step7.tsx or equivalent final step), after plan generation succeeds: if useUser().isSignedIn, call saveOnboardingProfile with onboarding state data
If user is not signed in, still save to sessionStorage as before (no regression)
Typecheck passes
Deploy and test: complete onboarding while signed in, then check /profile page shows the saved data

userStories.title: Add sign-out button and account info to profile page
userStories.description: As a signed-in user, I want to see my account email and sign out from the profile page.
acceptanceCriteria: In src/app/profile/page.tsx, import useUser and useClerk from @clerk/nextjs
At the top of profile page above 'Edit Your Profile' heading, add account section showing user.primaryEmailAddress?.emailAddress in text-sm var(--color-text-muted)
Add 'Sign Out' button below the email — onClick calls clerk.signOut() then router.push('/')
Account section only rendered when isSignedIn is true
Typecheck passes
Deploy and verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add subscription columns to users table
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to track subscription status, Stripe customer ID, and subscription ID per user.
acceptanceCriteria: Run migration: ALTER TABLE users ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS stripe_subscription_id TEXT
Run migration: ALTER TABLE users ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS sq_ft_source TEXT
Create src/lib/subscription.ts with getUserSubscription(clerkUserId: string): Promise<'free' | 'pro'> — queries users table, returns subscription_status cast to 'free' | 'pro'
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Install Stripe SDK and configure environment variables
userStories.description: As a developer, I need Stripe configured so payment flows can be built.
acceptanceCriteria: Install stripe package
Add env vars to .env.local: STRIPE_SECRET_KEY, STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET, STRIPE_PRO_PRICE_ID (placeholder values)
Update env.local.example with new Stripe key names
Create src/lib/stripe.ts exporting stripe singleton: import Stripe from 'stripe'; export const stripe = new Stripe(process.env.STRIPE_SECRET_KEY!)
Typecheck passes
Deploy to Vercel (Stripe test keys set in Vercel env vars)

userStories.title: Create Stripe checkout API route for Pro upgrade
userStories.description: As a user, I want to click 'Upgrade to Pro' and be taken to a Stripe checkout page.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/app/api/stripe/checkout/route.ts — POST handler
Handler calls auth() from Clerk; returns 401 JSON if not authenticated
Calls getOrCreateUser(userId, email) to get/create the user row
Creates or retrieves Stripe customer: if stripe_customer_id exists in DB use it; else call stripe.customers.create({ email }) and save the ID back to users table
Creates Stripe Checkout session: mode 'subscription', line_items with STRIPE_PRO_PRICE_ID qty 1, success_url '/dashboard?upgraded=1', cancel_url '/profile'
Returns { url: session.url } as JSON
Typecheck passes
Deploy; test manually by POSTing to the endpoint (returns a Stripe checkout URL)

userStories.title: Create Stripe webhook route to sync subscription status
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a webhook handler so the DB stays in sync when Stripe subscription events occur.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/app/api/stripe/webhook/route.ts — POST handler
Use request.text() to get raw body (NOT request.json()) before constructing the Stripe event
Verify webhook signature using stripe.webhooks.constructEvent(rawBody, sig, process.env.STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET!); return 400 on verification failure

Handle customer.subscription.created and customer.subscription.updated: if status === 'active', set subscription_status = 'pro' and store stripe_subscription_id in users table where stripe_customer_id matches
Handle customer.subscription.deleted: set subscription_status = 'free' in users table
Return { received: true } with status 200 on success
Typecheck passes
Deploy; note the production webhook URL for Stripe dashboard configuration

userStories.title: Create Stripe billing portal route for subscription management
userStories.description: As a Pro user, I want to manage my subscription from the profile page.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/app/api/stripe/portal/route.ts — POST handler
Get clerkUserId from auth(); look up stripe_customer_id from users table; return 400 if no customer ID found
Create Stripe billing portal session: stripe.billingPortal.sessions.create({ customer: customerId, return_url: process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL + '/profile' })
Return { url: session.url } as JSON
Add NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL to .env.local and env.local.example
Typecheck passes
Deploy and verify endpoint returns a portal URL

userStories.title: Add Pro feature gate helper and apply to future API routes
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a reusable gate so premium API routes consistently reject free users.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/lib/requirePro.ts exporting async function requirePro(clerkUserId: string): Promise<boolean> — calls getUserSubscription, returns true if 'pro'
Create src/actions/subscription.ts exporting getSubscriptionStatus() server action — calls auth() to get clerkUserId, calls getUserSubscription, returns 'free' | 'pro'
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add 'Upgrade to Pro' banner on dashboard for free users
userStories.description: As a free user on the dashboard, I want to see a prompt to upgrade so I know Pro features exist.
acceptanceCriteria: In src/app/dashboard/page.tsx, add subscriptionStatus state (default 'free') fetched via getSubscriptionStatus() server action in useEffect
When subscriptionStatus === 'free', show banner above the task list: text 'Unlock Pro: photo diagnosis + yard estimator' with 'Upgrade — $4.99/mo' button
Button click: POST to /api/stripe/checkout, on success window.location.href = data.url
Banner has var(--color-surface) background, 2px var(--color-primary) left border, is dismissible via X button (sets dismissed boolean in state)
When URL has ?upgraded=1 param: show toast 'Welcome to Pro!' for 3s and hide the banner
Typecheck passes
Deploy and verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add subscription section to profile page
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see my current plan and manage my subscription from the profile page.
acceptanceCriteria: In src/app/profile/page.tsx, add subscriptionStatus state loaded via getSubscriptionStatus() server action
Free users: show 'Free Plan' label + 'Upgrade to Pro — $4.99/mo' button (POST to /api/stripe/checkout then redirect to returned URL)
Pro users: show 'Pro Plan ✓' label in var(--color-primary) color + 'Manage Billing' button (POST to /api/stripe/portal then redirect)
Both buttons styled as outline: borderWidth 2px, borderColor var(--color-primary), color var(--color-primary), backgroundColor transparent
Plan section placed below the account info (email + sign-out) section
Typecheck passes
Deploy and verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add diagnoses table to database
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a table to store grass diagnosis history per user.
acceptanceCriteria: Run migration: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS diagnoses (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, clerk_user_id TEXT NOT NULL, image_url TEXT NOT NULL, grass_type TEXT, health_score INTEGER, issues JSONB, recommendations JSONB, raw_response TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW())
Create src/lib/diagnoses.ts with saveDiagnosis({ clerkUserId, imageUrl, grassType, healthScore, issues, recommendations }) and getDiagnoses(clerkUserId: string, limit?: number) functions
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create photo upload endpoint using Vercel Blob
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an endpoint that accepts an image file, stores it in Vercel Blob, and returns the URL.
acceptanceCriteria: Install @vercel/blob package
Add BLOB_READ_WRITE_TOKEN to env.local.example
Create src/app/api/diagnose/upload/route.ts — POST handler accepting multipart/form-data with a 'file' field
Validate file is image/jpeg or image/png; return 400 with { error: 'Invalid file type' } for other types
Require Clerk auth (auth() check); return 401 if not authenticated
Require Pro subscription (requirePro()); return 403 with { error: 'pro_required' } if free user
Call put(filename, file, { access: 'public' }) from @vercel/blob
Return { url: blobUrl } as JSON
Typecheck passes
Deploy to Vercel (BLOB_READ_WRITE_TOKEN set in Vercel env vars)

userStories.title: Create grass diagnosis API route using Claude Vision
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an API route that takes an image URL and returns Claude's analysis of the grass.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/app/api/diagnose/route.ts — POST handler accepting { imageUrl: string }
Require Clerk auth and Pro subscription; return 401/403 respectively if checks fail
Call Anthropic SDK (import Anthropic from 'anthropic'; use process.env.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY) with model claude-sonnet-4-6

Message structure: user message with image block { type: 'image', source: { type: 'url', url: imageUrl } } followed by text block with prompt asking for JSON: { grassType, healthScore (0-100), issues: string[], recommendations: string[] }

System prompt: 'You are a lawn care expert. Analyze this lawn photo and respond with valid JSON only, no markdown. Schema: { grassType: string, healthScore: number, issues: string[], recommendations: string[] }'
Parse response as JSON; if parsing fails return { error: 'Could not analyze image' } with status 422
Save result to diagnoses table via saveDiagnosis()
Return { grassType, healthScore, issues, recommendations } as JSON
Typecheck passes
Deploy and verify endpoint returns valid JSON for a test lawn image URL

userStories.title: Create /diagnose page with photo upload UI
userStories.description: As a Pro user, I want to upload a photo of my lawn and see diagnosis results.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/app/diagnose/page.tsx as a 'use client' component
Page heading 'Diagnose Your Lawn', subheading 'Upload a photo and get instant AI analysis'
Upload area: dashed border box (border-dashed, 2px, var(--color-border)), centered text 'Tap to upload or drag & drop', clicking opens file picker (accepts image/*)
On file select: show image preview via URL.createObjectURL, show 'Analyzing...' spinner overlay
Upload flow: POST file to /api/diagnose/upload → receive { url } → POST { imageUrl: url } to /api/diagnose → receive diagnosis
If /api/diagnose returns 403 with pro_required: show 'This feature requires Pro' message with 'Upgrade to Pro' button linking to /profile
On success: render diagnosis results (see US-075 for results card shape)
Typecheck passes
Deploy and verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Show diagnosis results card on /diagnose page
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see clear diagnosis results — grass type, health score, issues, and recommendations.
acceptanceCriteria: After diagnosis API returns success, render a results card below the upload area
Grass type row: 'Identified: {grassType}' in var(--color-primary) font-semibold

Health score bar: label 'Health Score {healthScore}/100', full-width track (var(--color-border) bg), filled portion (width: healthScore%, color: var(--color-primary) if >=70, var(--color-routine) if 40-69, var(--color-urgent) if <40), height 8px rounded-full
Issues section: heading 'Issues Found', bulleted list of issues[]; if empty show 'No issues detected ✓' in var(--color-primary)
Recommendations section: heading 'Recommendations', numbered list of recommendations[]
Results card: var(--color-surface) bg, var(--color-border) border, rounded-xl p-5, flex flex-col gap-4
'Diagnose Another' button below card resets state to show upload UI again
Typecheck passes
Deploy and verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add diagnosis history section to /diagnose page
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see my last 5 diagnoses so I can track changes over time.
acceptanceCriteria: Create getRecentDiagnoses() server action in src/actions/diagnose.ts — calls auth(), then getDiagnoses(clerkUserId, 5), returns array
At the bottom of /diagnose page, add 'Recent Diagnoses' section heading
Load diagnoses via the server action in a useEffect; store in state
Each history item: flex row with 40x40 rounded thumbnail (next/image or img with objectFit cover), grass_type text, health_score/100, date formatted as 'Jun 15'
If no diagnoses: show 'No diagnoses yet — upload your first photo above' in var(--color-text-muted)
After each new successful diagnosis, refetch the list
Typecheck passes
Deploy and verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add CameraIcon and Diagnose nav item to ClientNav
userStories.description: As a user, I want easy access to the diagnosis tool from the main navigation.
acceptanceCriteria: Add CameraIcon to src/components/icons.tsx: 20x20 viewBox SVG, camera body as rounded-rect, lens as circle, flash notch as small polygon on top — stroke='currentColor' fill='none'
Add fourth nav item to NAV_ITEMS in src/app/ClientNav.tsx: { label: 'Diagnose', href: '/diagnose', Icon: CameraIcon }
Mobile bottom tab bar now shows 4 items: Home, Calendar, Diagnose, Profile — each column is flex-1
Desktop nav shows all 4 links in the same order
Nav hidden on / and /onboarding/* as before
Typecheck passes
Deploy and verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Install Google Maps JS API loader and configure env
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the Google Maps SDK available so the yard estimator can use satellite imagery and drawing tools.
acceptanceCriteria: Install @googlemaps/js-api-loader package
Add NEXT_PUBLIC_GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY to .env.local (placeholder) and env.local.example

Create src/lib/maps.ts exporting loadGoogleMaps(): Promise<typeof google> — uses Loader from @googlemaps/js-api-loader with apiKey: process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY!, libraries: ['places', 'drawing', 'geometry']
Loader is a singleton — only calls load() once; subsequent calls return the same promise
Typecheck passes
Deploy to Vercel (real API key set in Vercel env vars with Maps JavaScript API, Places API, Drawing Library, Geometry Library enabled in Google Cloud Console)

userStories.title: Create /estimate page with address autocomplete and Pro gate
userStories.description: As a user, I want to type my address and have the map jump to my property.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/app/estimate/page.tsx as a 'use client' component
Page heading 'Estimate Your Yard Size', subheading 'Draw your yard on the map to calculate square footage'
Address input: <input type='text' placeholder='Enter your address...' /> styled with var(--color-surface) bg, var(--color-border) border, var(--color-text-primary) text, px-4 py-3 rounded-lg w-full
On mount: call loadGoogleMaps() then attach google.maps.places.Autocomplete to the input ref, restricted to types: ['address']
On place selection: extract lat/lng from place.geometry.location and store in state as { lat: number, lng: number } | null

Pro gate check: load subscriptionStatus via getSubscriptionStatus(); if 'free', show overlay div covering the map area with 'Pro feature — upgrade to access the yard estimator' and 'Upgrade to Pro' button linking to /profile
Typecheck passes
Deploy and verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Load Google Maps satellite view centered on selected address
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see a satellite view of my property after entering my address.
acceptanceCriteria: In src/app/estimate/page.tsx, add mapRef = useRef<HTMLDivElement>(null) and mapInstance = useRef<google.maps.Map | null>(null)
Map container: <div ref={mapRef} style={{ width: '100%', height: 400 }} className='rounded-xl overflow-hidden' />

When lat/lng state changes (address selected), initialize new google.maps.Map(mapRef.current, { center: { lat, lng }, zoom: 19, mapTypeId: 'satellite', tilt: 0, zoomControl: true, mapTypeControl: false, streetViewControl: false, fullscreenControl: false })
If map already initialized, call mapInstance.current.setCenter({ lat, lng }) instead of reinitializing
Before address selection, show placeholder div with 'Enter an address above to load the map' text in var(--color-text-muted) text-center py-16
Typecheck passes
Deploy and verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add polygon drawing tool to the yard estimator map
userStories.description: As a user, I want to draw the outline of my yard on the satellite map.
acceptanceCriteria: After map initializes, create google.maps.drawing.DrawingManager({ drawingMode: null, drawingControl: false, polygonOptions: { fillColor: '#4caf50', fillOpacity: 0.25, strokeColor: '#4caf50', strokeWeight: 2, editable: true } }) and call drawingManager.setMap(mapInstance.current)
Add 'Draw Yard' button below the map container — on click: remove existing polygon from map if any, call drawingManager.setDrawingMode(google.maps.drawing.OverlayType.POLYGON)
On polygoncomplete event: store polygon in useRef, call drawingManager.setDrawingMode(null), compute and display area (see US-082)
Add 'Clear' button (only shown when a polygon exists) — on click: polygon.setMap(null), reset area result state, reset polygonRef to null
Typecheck passes
Deploy and verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Calculate polygon area and display square footage result
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see the calculated square footage after drawing my yard polygon.
acceptanceCriteria: In the polygoncomplete handler, calculate area: const sqMeters = google.maps.geometry.spherical.computeArea(polygon.getPath()); const sqFt = Math.round(sqMeters * 10.7639 / 10) * 10
Store sqFt in useState<number | null>(null)

When sqFt is set, display below the map:

Estimated yard size: {sqFt.toLocaleString()} sq ft

in text-xl var(--color-text-primary)
Show 'Use this estimate →' button when sqFt is set (see US-083)
If user clears and redraws polygon, area updates to new value
Typecheck passes
Deploy and verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: 'Use this estimate' saves sq ft to profile and redirects
userStories.description: As a user, I want to save my estimated yard size to my profile in one click.
acceptanceCriteria: Create saveYardEstimate(sqFt: number) server action in src/actions/profile.ts
saveYardEstimate: gets clerkUserId from auth(), updates square_footage and sq_ft_source = 'maps_estimate' in users table WHERE clerk_user_id = clerkUserId
'Use this estimate →' button in /estimate page: calls saveYardEstimate(sqFt) then router.push('/profile?estimated=1')
Button shows 'Saving...' text while the action is in flight (use useState for loading)
Typecheck passes
Deploy and verify: click 'Use this estimate', confirm redirect to /profile with ?estimated=1 param

userStories.title: Show 'Estimated from map' badge and Re-estimate link on profile
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see when my yard size came from the map estimator and easily re-run it.
acceptanceCriteria: In src/app/profile/page.tsx, add sqFtSource state loaded from profile data (sq_ft_source column)

Below the Lawn Size input, when sqFtSource === 'maps_estimate': show

📍 Estimated from Google Maps

in text-xs var(--color-text-muted) + a 'Re-estimate →' link (href=/estimate) in text-xs var(--color-primary)
When user manually edits sqFt and saves, saveProfile sets sq_ft_source = 'manual' in the update
On /profile page with ?estimated=1 in URL, show toast 'Yard size saved from map estimate' for 3s then clear

userStories.title: Add lawns table and lawn CRUD functions
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a lawns table so each user can have multiple named properties.
acceptanceCriteria: Run migration: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS lawns (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, clerk_user_id TEXT NOT NULL, name TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'My Lawn', grass_type TEXT, state TEXT, zip_code TEXT, sq_ft INTEGER, sun_exposure TEXT, has_pets BOOLEAN DEFAULT false, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW())
Run migration: ALTER TABLE weekly_plans ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS lawn_id INTEGER

Create src/lib/lawns.ts exporting: getLawns(clerkUserId: string), getLawn(id: number), createLawn(data: { clerkUserId, name, grassType, state, zipCode, sqFt?, sunExposure?, hasPets? }), updateLawn(id: number, data: Partial<LawnData>) — all query the lawns table

userStories.title: Add lawn selector dropdown to dashboard header
userStories.description: As a user with multiple lawns, I want to switch between them from the dashboard.

onChange: setSelectedLawnId(Number(e.target.value)) — triggers plan reload for that lawn (existing loadData logic reads from selectedLawnId)

userStories.title: Create /lawns/new page to add a named lawn

Form fields: Lawn Name (text input, required, placeholder 'e.g. Front Yard'), ZIP Code (calls /api/zone on submit, shows zone result), Grass Type (same <select> as profile using grassTypes data), Sq Ft (number input, optional), Sun Exposure (Full Sun / Partial Shade / Full Shade button group), Has Pets (Yes / No button group)
All inputs use same styling as profile page (var(--color-surface) bg, var(--color-border) border, 2px borderWidth on button groups)

'Save Lawn' button calls addLawn(formData) then router.push('/dashboard')
'← Back to Dashboard' link at top of page

userStories.title: Add household_code to users table and invite/join API routes
userStories.description: As a developer, I need household invite infrastructure so users can share lawn access.
acceptanceCriteria: Run migration: ALTER TABLE users ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS household_code TEXT
Run migration: CREATE UNIQUE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS users_household_code_idx ON users(household_code) WHERE household_code IS NOT NULL

Create src/app/api/household/invite/route.ts — POST handler: get clerkUserId from auth(), if user already has household_code return it; else generate random 8-char alphanumeric code (Math.random().toString(36).slice(2,10).toUpperCase()), save to users table, return { code, inviteUrl: process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL + '/join/' + code }

Create src/app/api/household/join/route.ts — POST handler accepting { code: string }: find user with that household_code; if not found return 404; set the requesting user's household_code to the same value; return { success: true }

Update getLawns in src/lib/lawns.ts: also return lawns from users who share the same household_code (JOIN users ON lawns.clerk_user_id = users.clerk_user_id WHERE users.household_code = (SELECT household_code FROM users WHERE clerk_user_id = $1))
Typecheck passes
Deploy to Vercel

userStories.title: Add household sharing UI to profile page
userStories.description: As a user, I want to invite household members from the profile page.
acceptanceCriteria: In src/app/profile/page.tsx, add 'Household' section below the Plan section
'Share with household' button: on click POST to /api/household/invite, store returned inviteUrl in state

When inviteUrl is set: show <input readOnly value={inviteUrl} /> styled with var(--color-surface) bg + 'Copy Link' button that calls navigator.clipboard.writeText(inviteUrl) and shows 'Copied!' toast for 2s

Create src/app/join/[code]/page.tsx: client component that on mount POSTs { code } to /api/household/join, on success calls router.push('/dashboard?joined=1'), on 404 shows 'Invalid invite link' message
On /dashboard with ?joined=1 param, show toast 'You've joined the household — shared lawns are now visible' for 3s
Typecheck passes
Deploy and verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add /estimate link to navigation and profile quick-actions
userStories.description: As a user, I want easy access to the yard estimator from navigation and profile.
acceptanceCriteria: Add 'Map' or 'Estimate' entry to the bottom nav as a fifth item — OR add it as a sub-link only visible on /profile under the lawn size field (preferred to avoid overcrowding the 4-item bottom nav)

On /profile page, below the Lawn Size input (next to the 'Estimated from map' badge if present, or as a standalone link if sq_ft_source is not 'maps_estimate'): show 'Estimate with Google Maps →' link (href=/estimate) in text-sm var(--color-primary)
In the landing page 'Why LawnGuide?' features section or 'How it works' section: add a mention of the yard estimator as a feature (update one card or step to reference it)
Typecheck passes
Deploy and verify in browser using dev-browser skill
